pages.full_text:
86 WestField Rd, Edgbaston, Birmingham
B15 3 JG.      0121-454-3730.
2005. July – 2008 July.
pages.notes: This appears to be an inside cover or flyleaf page with an ownership/address label and a date-range label for the volume section (2005 July – 2008 July). The text in the bottom-right corner ('Tru...', 'Sin...', 'Wo...') is the 3% overlap bleed from the right page caption and has not been transcribed here. Left margin shows the edge of a decorative or photo element from the facing page.

pages.full_text:
Three rabbits representing the
Trinity, Mediaeval glass, Long Melford.
Similar representation found in [?6] China &
wooden roof bosses in Devon.
photo_captions: Three rabbits representing the Trinity, Mediaeval glass, Long Melford. Similar representation found in [?6] China & wooden roof bosses in Devon.
pages.notes: Page contains a photograph of stained glass depicting three hares/rabbits (a medieval 'three hares' motif). The handwritten caption below reads as transcribed. The '[?6]' reading is uncertain — could be a stray numeral or possibly 'in' written with a flourish, or an abbreviation. 'China' here likely refers to the country China, not a place in Britain. Long Melford is a village in Suffolk, UK.
